character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A man in his late 40s or early 50s, with a stern and serious expression, wearing an expensive, dark business suit. He has short, neat hair. East Asian ethnicity.

hanzi: 21世纪初以来,中国的经济有了很大的发展,不少跨国公司进入中国。
这些大公司吸引了世界各地不少的人材，包括一些在国外学习毕业的中国留学生。
他们从海外归来，被叫做“海归”。
有的人还开玩笑地把他们叫做“海龟”。
林雪梅现在就是其中的一只新“海龟”。
pinyin: èr shí yī shì jì chū yǐ lái zhōng guó de jīng jì yǒu le hěn dà de fā zhǎn bù shǎo kuà guó gōng sī jìn rù zhōng guó
zhè xiē dà gōng sī xī yǐn le shì jiè gè dì bù shǎo de rén cái bāo kuò yī xiē zài guó wài xué xí bì yè de zhōng guó liú xué shēng
tā men cóng hǎi wài guī lái bèi jiào zuò hǎi guī
yǒu de rén hái kāi wánxiào de bǎ tāmen jiàozuò hǎiguī
Lín Xuěméi xiànzài jiùshì qízhōng de yì zhī xīn hǎiguī
translation: Since the beginning of the 21st century, China's economy has developed greatly, and many multinational corporations have entered China.
These large companies have attracted a lot of talent from all over the world, including some Chinese students who graduated from their studies abroad.
When they return from overseas, they are called 'hǎiguī' (returnees).
Some people also jokingly call them 'hǎiguī' (sea turtles).
Lin Xuemei is now one of these new 'sea turtles'.

scenes.scene_goal: 介绍时代背景、“海归”现象，并引出主角林雪梅。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 快速蒙太奇镜头展示中国现代化都市的繁荣景象，跨国公司大楼林立。接着画面切换，展现一群年轻的中国留学生在国外毕业，然后乘飞机回到中国。最后，镜头聚焦于主角林雪梅，她拖着行李箱走出机场，一个可爱的卡通海龟图标在她头边弹出。
scenes.setting_en: Montage of modern Chinese cityscapes, a Western university campus, and an airport arrival hall.
scenes.camera_movement: Fast cuts, followed by a tracking shot.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Montage sequence: fast-paced shots of a modern Chinese city skyline like Shanghai with skyscrapers featuring logos of multinational corporations. Quick cut to young Chinese students in graduation gowns on a western university campus. Cut to an airplane landing. Finally, a tracking shot follows Lin Xuemei walking out of an airport arrival hall, pulling a suitcase. A cute cartoon sea turtle icon with a graduation cap briefly appears and floats beside her head with a 'pop' sound effect.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 21世纪初以来,中国的经济有了很大的发展,不少跨国公司进入中国。这些大公司吸引了世界各地不少的人材，包括一些在国外学习毕业的中国留学生。他们从海外归来，被叫做“海归”。有的人还开玩笑地把他们叫做“海龟”。林雪梅现在就是其中的一只新“海龟”。
voice_over.pinyin: èr shí yī shì jì chū yǐ lái zhōng guó de jīng jì yǒu le hěn dà de fā zhǎn bù shǎo kuà guó gōng sī jìn rù zhōng guó. zhè xiē dà gōng sī xī yǐn le shì jiè gè dì bù shǎo de rén cái bāo kuò yī xiē zài guó wài xué xí bì yè de zhōng guó liú xué shēng. tā men cóng hǎi wài guī lái bèi jiào zuò hǎi guī. yǒu de rén hái kāi wánxiào de bǎ tāmen jiàozuò hǎiguī. Lín Xuěméi xiànzài jiùshì qízhōng de yì zhī xīn hǎiguī.
voice_over.translation: Since the beginning of the 21st century, China's economy has developed greatly, and many multinational corporations have entered China. These large companies have attracted a lot of talent from all over the world, including some Chinese students who graduated from their studies abroad. When they return from overseas, they are called 'hǎiguī' (returnees). Some people also jokingly call them 'hǎiguī' (sea turtles). Lin Xuemei is now one of these new 'sea turtles'.

hanzi: 雪梅回到中国以后不久,就开始上网查资料、找工作。
她去过几家公司实习,但是都不太满意。
昨天有一家她非常喜欢的大公司叫她今天上午去面试销售经理的工作。
pinyin: Xuěméi huídào Zhōngguó yǐhòu bùjiǔ jiù kāishǐ shàngwǎng chá zīliào zhǎo gōngzuò
tā qùguò jǐ jiā gōngsī shíxí dànshì dōu bù tài mǎnyì
zuótiān yǒu yī jiā tā fēicháng xǐhuān de dà gōngsī jiào tā jīntiān shàngwǔ qù miànshì xiāoshòu jīnglǐ de gōngzuò
translation: Not long after returning to China, Xuemei started searching for information and looking for a job online.
She had interned at several companies, but wasn't very satisfied with any of them.
Yesterday, a large company that she really likes asked her to come in this morning for an interview for a sales manager position.

scenes.scene_goal: 展示雪梅的求职过程和即将到来的重要面试机会。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 雪梅坐在家里的书桌前，专注地浏览着笔记本电脑上的招聘网站。快速切换几个她去不同公司实习的镜头，她看起来都不太满意。最后，她的脸上闪过一丝喜悦，鼠标点击了一个著名公司的“销售经理”招聘广告。
scenes.setting_en: Lin Xuemei's modern apartment, with quick cuts to various office environments.
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot, quick cuts, zoom in.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium shot of Lin Xuemei at her desk, intently scrolling through job websites on her laptop. Quick cuts of her in different office settings for internships, looking disappointed. Back at her laptop, her eyes light up. Camera zooms in on the screen as her mouse cursor clicks on a job posting for 'Sales Manager' from a major company.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 雪梅回到中国以后不久,就开始上网查资料、找工作。她去过几家公司实习,但是都不太满意。昨天有一家她非常喜欢的大公司叫她今天上午去面试销售经理的工作。
voice_over.pinyin: Xuěméi huídào Zhōngguó yǐhòu bùjiǔ jiù kāishǐ shàngwǎng chá zīliào zhǎo gōngzuò. tā qùguò jǐ jiā gōngsī shíxí dànshì dōu bù tài mǎnyì. zuótiān yǒu yī jiā tā fēicháng xǐhuān de dà gōngsī jiào tā jīntiān shàngwǔ qù miànshì xiāoshòu jīnglǐ de gōngzuò.
voice_over.translation: Not long after returning to China, Xuemei started searching for information and looking for a job online. She had interned at several companies, but wasn't very satisfied with any of them. Yesterday, a large company that she really likes asked her to come in this morning for an interview for a sales manager position.

hanzi: 面试以后,刚回到家里就接到丽莎的电话。
哎,“海龟”小姐,今天的面试怎么样?
哈,开始有些紧张,出了好多汗,套装的上衣都快湿了。
你已经不是第一次面试了,干吗那么紧张?
pinyin: miànshì yǐhòu gāng huídào jiālǐ jiù jiēdào Lìshā de diànhuà
āi hǎiguī xiǎojiě jīntiān de miànshì zěnmeyàng
hā kāishǐ yǒuxiē jǐnzhāng chū le hǎoduō hàn tàozhuāng de shàngyī dōu kuài shī le
nǐ yǐjīng búshì dì yī cì miànshì le gànmá nàme jǐnzhāng
translation: After the interview, she had just gotten home when she received a call from Lisa.
Hey, Ms. 'Sea Turtle,' how was your interview today?
Ha, I was a bit nervous at the beginning. I sweated so much that the top of my suit was almost soaked.
This isn't your first interview. Why were you so nervous?

scenes.scene_goal: 表现雪梅面试后的疲惫状态，并通过与朋友的通话，引出她紧张的心情。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 雪梅穿着职业套装，略显疲惫地回到公寓。她刚把包扔在沙发上，手机就响了，来电显示是“丽莎”。她接起电话，朋友开玩笑地问候她。雪梅叹了口气，一边解开西装外套的扣子，一边承认自己很紧张，甚至紧张到出汗。
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot, then a close-up on the phone.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Lin Xuemei, looking tired in her business suit, enters her apartment. Her phone rings. Close-up on the phone screen showing caller ID '丽莎 (Lisa)'. She answers. We hear Lisa's cheerful voice. Xuemei sighs, unbuttoning her blazer, and confesses how nervous she was, wiping her brow theatrically.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白, 丽莎, 雪梅, 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 面试以后,刚回到家里就接到丽莎的电话。哎,“海龟”小姐,今天的面试怎么样? 哈,开始有些紧张,出了好多汗,套装的上衣都快湿了。你已经不是第一次面试了,干吗那么紧张?
voice_over.pinyin: miànshì yǐhòu gāng huídào jiālǐ jiù jiēdào Lìshā de diànhuà. āi hǎiguī xiǎojiě jīntiān de miànshì zěnmeyàng? hā kāishǐ yǒuxiē jǐnzhāng chū le hǎoduō hàn tàozhuāng de shàngyī dōu kuài shī le. nǐ yǐjīng búshì dì yī cì miànshì le gànmá nàme jǐnzhāng?
voice_over.translation: After the interview, she had just gotten home when she received a call from Lisa. Hey, Ms. 'Sea Turtle,' how was your interview today? Ha, I was a bit nervous at the beginning. I sweated so much that the top of my suit was almost soaked. This isn't your first interview. Why were you so nervous?

hanzi: 给我面试的是他们公司的总经理,挺严肃的,第一句话就是:“你是不是在美国找不到工作才回中国来的?”
这个总经理可真的挺吓人的!难怪你紧张。
这个总经理可真的挺吓人的!难怪你紧张。
pinyin: gěi wǒ miànshì de shì tāmen gōngsī de zǒngjīnglǐ tǐng yánsù de dìyī jù huà jiùshì nǐ shì bu shì zài Měiguó zhǎo bú dào gōngzuò cái huí Zhōngguó lái de
zhège zǒngjīnglǐ kě zhēnde tǐng xiàrén de nánguài nǐ jǐnzhāng
zhège zǒngjīnglǐ kě zhēnde tǐng xiàrén de nánguài nǐ jǐnzhāng
translation: The person who interviewed me was their general manager. He was very stern. His first sentence was: 'Did you come back to China because you couldn't find a job in the United States?'
This general manager is really scary! No wonder you were nervous.
This general manager is really scary! No wonder you were nervous.

scenes.scene_goal: 通过闪回揭示雪梅紧张的原因——面试官的严厉和尖锐问题，并以朋友的反应作结。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 雪梅继续在电话里描述。画面闪回到面试场景：雪梅坐在一个豪华但气氛严肃的办公室里，对面是一位表情严厉的总经理。总经理用审视的目光看着她，说出那句尖锐的问题。闪回结束，我们听到电话里丽莎惊讶又同情的声音，雪梅脸上露出一丝苦笑。
scenes.setting_en: Lin Xuemei's living room, with a flashback to a corporate manager's office.
scenes.camera_movement: Quick cut to flashback, close-up, then cut back.
scenes.video_prompt_en: As Xuemei speaks on the phone, the scene flashes back to the interview. Xuemei sits nervously in a large, intimidating office across from a stern General Manager. Extreme close-up on the manager's severe face as he asks his sharp question. Flashback ends. We are back with Xuemei on the phone, hearing Lisa's sympathetic reaction. Xuemei gives a wry smile.

voice_over.speaker: 雪梅, 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 给我面试的是他们公司的总经理,挺严肃的,第一句话就是:“你是不是在美国找不到工作才回中国来的?” 这个总经理可真的挺吓人的!难怪你紧张。
voice_over.pinyin: gěi wǒ miànshì de shì tāmen gōngsī de zǒngjīnglǐ tǐng yánsù de dìyī jù huà jiùshì nǐ shì bu shì zài Měiguó zhǎo bú dào gōngzuò cái huí Zhōngguó lái de? zhège zǒngjīnglǐ kě zhēnde tǐng xiàrén de nánguài nǐ jǐnzhāng.
voice_over.translation: The person who interviewed me was their general manager. He was very stern. His first sentence was: 'Did you come back to China because you couldn't find a job in the United States?' This general manager is really scary! No wonder you were nervous.

hanzi: 还没呢!这时候总经理突然笑着问:“你觉得你自己最大的缺点是什么?”
pinyin: hái méi ne zhè shí hou zǒng jīng lǐ tū rán xiào zhe wèn nǐ jué de nǐ zì jǐ zuì dà de quē diǎn shì shén me
translation: Not yet! Just then, the general manager suddenly smiled and asked, 'What do you think is your biggest weakness?'

scenes.scene_goal: 引入面试中的关键转折问题，展示面试官态度的变化，制造悬念。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 【闪回-面试办公室】雪梅正在讲述面试过程。画面闪回到面试现场，总经理脸上的表情已经由严肃转为欣赏，他身体微微前倾，带着一丝笑容，向雪梅提出了一个出人意料的问题。雪梅的表情从自信转为一丝惊讶。
scenes.setting_en: Flashback to a modern, bright office. The General Manager and Xuemei are sitting across a desk from each other.
scenes.camera_movement: Slow zoom in on the General Manager's face as he smiles and asks the question.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Flashback scene. In a bright, modern office, a middle-aged Chinese general manager in a suit leans forward with a slight smile, looking at a young female applicant (Xuemei). The manager asks a question. Xuemei's confident expression shifts to one of slight surprise. Cinematic, soft lighting.

voice_over.speaker: 雪梅
voice_over.hanzi: 还没呢!这时候总经理突然笑着问:“你觉得你自己最大的缺点是什么?”
voice_over.pinyin: hái méi ne zhè shí hou zǒng jīng lǐ tū rán xiào zhe wèn nǐ jué de nǐ zì jǐ zuì dà de quē diǎn shì shén me
voice_over.translation: Not yet! Just then, the general manager suddenly smiled and asked, 'What do you think is your biggest weakness?'

hanzi: 哈,我觉得,你最大的缺点就是爱吃零食。
那他不是要把我看成小女孩了?
那你是怎么回答的?
pinyin: hā wǒ jué de nǐ zuì dà de quē diǎn jiù shì ài chī líng shí
nà tā bú shì yào bǎ wǒ kàn chéng xiǎo nǚ hái le
nà nǐ shì zěnme huídá de
translation: Ha, I think your biggest weakness is that you love to eat snacks.
Wouldn't he see me as a little girl then?
So how did you answer?

scenes.scene_goal: 通过朋友间的玩笑互动，缓和紧张气氛，同时增强观众对雪梅真实回答的好奇心。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 【现实-咖啡馆】画面切回咖啡馆。丽莎听到这个问题，夸张地大笑起来，然后开玩笑说雪梅的缺点是爱吃零食，还做出一个吃东西的滑稽动作。雪梅无奈地白了她一眼反驳。丽莎立刻收起笑容，身体前倾，好奇地追问她到底是怎么回答的。
scenes.setting_en: Back in the cozy cafe. Xuemei and Lisa are sitting at a table with coffee cups.
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot, with quick cuts between Lisa's playful face and Xuemei's amused-but-annoyed reaction.
scenes.shot_type: Two-shot, followed by over-the-shoulder shots for the dialogue exchange.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cut back to a cozy cafe. Two young women, Lisa and Xuemei, are talking. Lisa laughs out loud, playfully mimics eating a snack. Xuemei rolls her eyes and retorts with a smile. Lisa then leans in, her expression turning curious and eager. Natural light from a large window.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 哈,我觉得,你最大的缺点就是爱吃零食。
voice_over.pinyin: hā wǒ jué de nǐ zuì dà de quē diǎn jiù shì ài chī líng shí
voice_over.translation: Ha, I think your biggest weakness is that you love to eat snacks.

hanzi: 我说,一个好的销售管理人材,应该能科学地安排自己的时间,善于工作的人往往也是善于休息的,我在这方面还很不够。实习的时候,我工作一有压力就熬夜加班。所以,不太会休息可能是我最大的缺点。
雪梅,你到底是在说自己的缺点还是优点啊?
pinyin: wǒ shuō yí ge hǎo de xiāoshòu guǎnlǐ réncái yīnggāi néng kēxué de ānpái zìjǐ de shíjiān shànyú gōngzuò de rén wǎngwǎng yě shì shànyú xiūxi de wǒ zài zhè fāngmiàn hái hěn bú gòu shíxí de shíhou wǒ gōngzuò yì yǒu yālì jiù áoyè jiābān suǒyǐ bú tài huì xiūxi kěnéng shì wǒ zuìdà de quēdiǎn
Xuěméi nǐ dàodǐ shì zài shuō zìjǐ de quēdiǎn háishì yōudiǎn a
translation: I said, a good sales management professional should be able to schedule their time scientifically. People who are good at working are often also good at resting. I'm not quite good enough in this aspect. During my internship, whenever I was under pressure, I would stay up late and work overtime. So, not being good at taking breaks is probably my biggest weakness.
Xuemei, are you actually talking about your weakness or your strength?

scenes.scene_goal: 展示雪梅如何巧妙地回答难题，将缺点转化为优点，体现她的职场智慧。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 【闪回-面试办公室/交叉剪辑】闪回至面试现场，雪梅镇定自若地回答问题。当她提到“熬夜加班”时，画面快速闪过一个她过去在办公室深夜工作的蒙太奇镜头。然后切回咖啡馆，丽莎听完后，露出既佩服又觉得好笑的表情，问她这到底算缺点还是优点。
scenes.setting_en: Flashback to the office, with a quick montage, then cut back to the cafe.
scenes.camera_movement: Slow push-in on Xuemei's face as she speaks. Quick cuts for the montage. Cut to Lisa's amazed face.
scenes.shot_type: Medium close-up, montage sequence, medium shot.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Flashback: Xuemei sits confidently in the interview, explaining her 'weakness'. Quick montage sequence: Xuemei working late in an office, looking tired but determined, screen glowing on her face. Cut back to the cafe: Lisa looks at Xuemei with a mix of awe and amusement, smiling as she asks her question.

voice_over.speaker: 雪梅
voice_over.hanzi: 我说,一个好的销售管理人材...不太会休息可能是我最大的缺点。
voice_over.pinyin: wǒ shuō, yí ge hǎo de xiāoshòu guǎnlǐ réncái...bú tài huì xiūxi kěnéng shì wǒ zuìdà de quēdiǎn.
voice_over.translation: I said, a good sales management professional...not being good at taking breaks is probably my biggest weakness.

hanzi: 我又说,只要我们能把缺点变成优点,缺点就没那么可怕了。说到这儿,总经理站起来,一边跟我握手,一边笑着说,他相信我的能力,让我等他们的好消息。
太棒了!恭喜你,林经理!
pinyin: wǒ yòu shuō zhǐyào wǒmen néng bǎ quēdiǎn biànchéng yōudiǎn quēdiǎn jiù méi nàme kěpà le shuōdào zhèr zǒngjīnglǐ zhànqilai yībiān gēn wǒ wòshǒu yībiān xiào zhe shuō tā xiāngxìn wǒ de nénglì ràng wǒ děng tāmen de hǎo xiāoxi
tài bàng le gōngxǐ nǐ lín jīnglǐ
translation: I added that as long as we can turn our weaknesses into strengths, weaknesses are not so terrible. At this point, the general manager stood up, shook my hand, and said with a smile that he believed in my abilities and that I should wait for their good news.
That's great! Congratulations, Manager Lin!

scenes.scene_goal: 以面试成功和朋友的祝贺圆满结束故事，营造积极、励志的氛围。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 【闪回-面试办公室/现实-咖啡馆】闪回面试的最后一刻。雪梅补充完最后一句。总经理满意地站起来，主动与雪梅握手，脸上是欣赏的笑容。画面切回咖啡馆，丽莎兴奋地举起咖啡杯，像是在敬酒，开心地向雪梅道贺：“恭喜你，林经理！”雪梅灿烂地笑了。
scenes.setting_en: Final flashback to the office, concluding in the cafe.
scenes.camera_movement: Camera follows the General Manager as he stands and extends his hand. Close-up on the handshake. Cut to a joyful shot of Lisa raising her cup in the cafe.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Final flashback: The general manager stands up, smiling warmly, and shakes Xuemei's hand firmly. Close-up on the handshake, symbolizing success. Cut back to the cafe: Lisa joyfully raises her coffee cup in a toast, exclaiming 'Congratulations, Manager Lin!'. Xuemei beams with happiness and relief. The atmosphere is celebratory and warm.

voice_over.speaker: 雪梅
voice_over.hanzi: 说到这儿,总经理站起来,一边跟我握手,一边笑着说,他相信我的能力,让我等他们的好消息。
voice_over.pinyin: shuōdào zhèr zǒngjīnglǐ zhànqilai yībiān gēn wǒ wòshǒu yībiān xiào zhe shuō tā xiāngxìn wǒ de nénglì ràng wǒ děng tāmen de hǎo xiāoxi
voice_over.translation: At this point, the general manager stood up, shook my hand, and said with a smile that he believed in my abilities and that I should wait for their good news.

global_config.teaching_style: A friendly and encouraging guide, breaking down language and culture into understandable pieces. The tone is supportive, aiming to build student confidence by explaining not just what to say, but why it's said that way.
global_config.visual_style: Clean, modern, and card-based, using animations to highlight characters, words, and sentence structures. Visuals should support the explanation, not distract from it.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the context of China's economic growth and the influx of multinational companies, and to learn related vocabulary.

narration.subtitle_en: This first sentence sets the scene for our story. It says that since the beginning of the 21st century, China's economy has developed greatly. Let's break it down. [zh:21世纪初以来] means 'since the beginning of the 21st century'. [zh:经济] is 'economy', and [zh:发展] is 'development'. As a result, many [zh:跨国公司], or 'multinational corporations', have [zh:进入] 'entered' China. This economic boom created many new job opportunities.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 21世纪初以来,中国的经济有了很大的发展,不少跨国公司进入中国。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: 21 shìjì chū yǐlái, Zhōngguó de jīngjì yǒule hěn dà de fāzhǎn, bù shǎo kuàguó gōngsī jìnrù Zhōngguó.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Since the beginning of the 21st century, China's economy has developed greatly, and many multinational corporations have entered China.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to a period of 100 years.
An adjective describing something that operates across different countries. It's formed by [zh:跨] (to cross) and [zh:国] (country).
A verb meaning to go into or get into a place or state.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a timeline starting from '2000'. Show logos of international companies appearing on a map of China.

segments.teaching_goal: To explain who was attracted by these companies and introduce vocabulary for talent and overseas students.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence explains who was drawn to these new jobs. It says these large companies have [zh:吸引] 'attracted' a lot of [zh:人材] 'talent' from [zh:世界各地] 'all over the world'. This group includes Chinese students who studied [zh:在国外] 'abroad' and graduated. The word [zh:留学生] specifically means 'students studying abroad' or 'overseas students'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这些大公司吸引了世界各地不少的人材，包括一些在国外学习毕业的中国留学生。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhèxiē dà gōngsī xīyǐnle shìjiè gèdì bùshǎo de réncái, bāokuò yīxiē zài guówài xuéxí bìyè de Zhōngguó liúxuéshēng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: These large companies have attracted a lot of talent from all over the world, including some Chinese students who graduated from their studies abroad.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb meaning to draw in or pull towards.
A person of ability or talent. We'll look at this word more closely next.
Literally 'outside the country', it means foreign countries or overseas.

segments.visual_notes: Animation showing people from different parts of a world map moving towards China.

segments.segment_title: Vocabulary: 人材 vs. 人才
segments.teaching_goal: To understand the interchangeability and subtle difference between 人材 and 人才.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's look closer at the word for 'talent'. In the text, you see [zh:人材]. You might also see it written as [zh:人才]. For all practical purposes, they are interchangeable. The character [zh:材] literally means 'timber' or 'material', suggesting a person is a valuable resource for a company or country. The character [zh:才] means 'talent' or 'ability' directly. Both refer to a talented person.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 人材 / 人才
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: réncái / réncái
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: person of ability, talent
on_screen_text.notes: 材 (cái) means 'material', while 才 (cái) means 'talent'. Both are used.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A person of ability. Can also be written as 人才.

segments.visual_notes: Show the two words side-by-side, highlighting the different second character in each.

segments.teaching_goal: To introduce and explain the modern Chinese term '海归' (hǎiguī) for overseas returnees.

narration.subtitle_en: This line introduces a very important modern Chinese term. It says when these students [zh:归来] 'return' from [zh:海外] 'overseas', they are [zh:被叫做] 'called' [zh:海归]. This word is a clever combination of [zh:海] from 'overseas' and [zh:归] from 'return'. It specifically refers to Chinese people who have returned to China after studying or working abroad.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他们从海外归来，被叫做“海归”。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tāmen cóng hǎiwài guīlái, bèi jiàozuò “hǎiguī”.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: When they return from overseas, they are called 'hǎiguī' (returnees).

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'outside the seas', it means abroad or overseas.
A formal way to say 'to come back' or 'to return'.
Used to introduce a name or a term for something.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the characters 海 from 海外 and 归 from 归来 combining to form the word 海归.

segments.teaching_goal: To introduce the pun '海龟' (sea turtle) and understand its connection to '海归'.

narration.subtitle_en: Now for a bit of Chinese humor. The text says some people jokingly call them [zh:海龟]. Why? Because the word for 'sea turtle', [zh:海龟], is pronounced exactly the same as the word for 'returnee', [zh:海归]. This is a pun, a common and fun aspect of the Chinese language. The phrase [zh:开玩笑] means 'to joke' or 'to kid'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 有的人还开玩笑地把他们叫做“海龟”。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yǒu de rén hái kāi wánxiào de bǎ tāmen jiàozuò “hǎiguī”.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Some people also jokingly call them 'hǎiguī' (sea turtles).

highlight_words.explanation_en: A pun on '海归' (returnee) because they have the same pronunciation.
A common phrase meaning to make a joke or to kid around.

segments.visual_notes: Show the word 海归 morphing into an image of a sea turtle (海龟) with the pinyin 'hǎiguī' displayed prominently.

segments.segment_title: Usage: 海归 vs. 海龟
segments.teaching_goal: To understand the cultural context of the '海龟' pun and its opposite, '土鳖'.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's dive deeper into this pun. [zh:海归] refers to a highly educated person returning from overseas. The pun [zh:海龟], meaning 'sea turtle', is a funny and widely used nickname. There's even an opposite term: [zh:土鳖], which literally means 'rustic soft-shelled turtle'. People who have never studied abroad might use this term to describe themselves in a self-deprecating way. It's a great example of modern Chinese slang.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 海归 (hǎiguī) vs. 海龟 (hǎiguī)
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Returnee vs. Sea Turtle (Pun)
on_screen_text.notes: Opposite: 土鳖 (tǔbiē) - 'rustic turtle', someone who hasn't been abroad.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A person who has returned from overseas, typically after studying.
A pun on '海归' due to identical pronunciation.
Literally 'soft-shelled turtle', a self-deprecating slang term for someone who has never studied abroad, the opposite of a '海归'.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a person with a graduation cap and suitcase (海归) next to an icon of a sea turtle (海龟). Then introduce the '土鳖' icon, perhaps a turtle staying on land.

segments.teaching_goal: To introduce the main character, Lin Xuemei, and understand how the 'sea turtle' pun is applied to her.

narration.subtitle_en: Here we meet our main character. The sentence says, 'Lin Xuemei is now one of these new sea turtles.' Notice the measure word used here: [zh:一只]. This measure word is typically used for animals. Using it here with [zh:海龟] reinforces the 'sea turtle' pun and adds to the playful tone of the description.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 林雪梅现在就是其中的一只新“海龟”。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lín Xuěméi xiànzài jiùshì qízhōng de yì zhī xīn “hǎiguī”.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Lin Xuemei is now one of these new 'sea turtles'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A word used to mean 'among' or 'in which'.
A measure word, often used for animals, which playfully reinforces the 'sea turtle' pun.

segments.visual_notes: Show a portrait of Lin Xuemei with a small, cute sea turtle icon next to her name.

segments.teaching_goal: To describe Lin Xuemei's actions after returning to China and learn vocabulary related to job searching.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence describes what Xuemei did after coming back to China. [zh:以后不久] means 'not long after'. So, not long after returning, she immediately started her job search. The sentence lists three key actions: [zh:上网] 'to go online', [zh:查资料] 'to search for information', and [zh:找工作] 'to look for a job'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 雪梅回到中国以后不久,就开始上网查资料、找工作。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xuěméi huídào Zhōngguó yǐhòu bùjiǔ, jiù kāishǐ shàngwǎng chá zīliào, zhǎo gōngzuò.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Not long after returning to China, Xuemei started searching for information and looking for a job online.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Means 'soon' or 'not long after'.
Literally 'to get on the net', this is the standard term for using the internet.
Refers to materials, data, or information.

segments.visual_notes: Animate three icons in sequence: 1. A computer screen (上网), 2. A magnifying glass (查资料), 3. A briefcase (找工作).

segments.teaching_goal: To explain Xuemei's past internship experience and her feelings about it.

narration.subtitle_en: This line tells us about her initial job-seeking experience. She had interned at several companies, which is [zh:实习]. The particle [zh:过] after the verb indicates a past experience. However, the sentence says she [zh:都不太满意], meaning she 'wasn't very satisfied with any of them'. The key word here is [zh:满意], which means 'satisfied'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 她去过几家公司实习,但是都不太满意。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā qùguo jǐ jiā gōngsī shíxí, dànshì dōu bú tài mǎnyì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: She had interned at several companies, but wasn't very satisfied with any of them.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To work as an intern; an internship.
An adjective or verb meaning to be pleased or satisfied. Here it's negated with [zh:不太] to mean 'not very satisfied'.

segments.visual_notes: Show Xuemei's face with a neutral or slightly unhappy expression, with logos of different companies in the background.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the details of the job interview opportunity Xuemei received.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence is a bit long, but it contains the key event. Let's break it down. 'Yesterday, a large company that she really likes...' ([zh:昨天有一家她非常喜欢的大公司]). '...asked her to come in this morning for an interview...' ([zh:叫她今天上午去面试]). The job is for a [zh:销售经理] 'sales manager'. The word [zh:面试] is 'job interview', a very important word for this lesson.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 昨天有一家她非常喜欢的大公司叫她今天上午去面试销售经理的工作。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zuótiān yǒu yī jiā tā fēicháng xǐhuān de dà gōngsī jiào tā jīntiān shàngwǔ qù miànshì xiāoshòu jīnglǐ de gōngzuò.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Yesterday, a large company that she really likes asked her to come in this morning for an interview for a sales manager position.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'face test', this is the word for a job interview.
The act of selling or marketing products.
A common job title for a manager or director.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a calendar flipping to 'today'. Show an icon of a building with a heart above it, then an arrow pointing to Xuemei, with the words '面试' appearing.

segments.teaching_goal: To set the scene for the dialogue and understand the '刚...就...' structure.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence sets the timing for the phone call. [zh:面试以后] means 'after the interview'. The structure [zh:刚...就...] is very useful. It means 'as soon as... then...'. So, 'as soon as she got home, she received a call from Lisa'. The verb phrase [zh:接到电话] means 'to receive a phone call'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 面试以后,刚回到家里就接到丽莎的电话。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Miànshì yǐhòu, gāng huídào jiālǐ jiù jiēdào Lìshā de diànhuà.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: After the interview, she had just gotten home when she received a call from Lisa.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure indicating that the second action happens immediately after the first.
Means to receive something, like a call or a letter. [zh:接] is 'to connect' and [zh:到] indicates successful completion.

segments.visual_notes: Show Xuemei walking through her front door, and immediately her phone rings with '丽莎' on the screen.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand Lisa's opening question and the use of '怎么样'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here the dialogue begins. Lisa starts with [zh:哎], a casual interjection like 'Hey!'. She playfully calls Xuemei '[zh:海龟]小姐', or 'Ms. Sea Turtle'. Then she asks the main question: [zh:今天的面试怎么样?], which means 'How was your interview today?'. The phrase [zh:怎么样] is a very common and useful way to ask for an opinion or to ask how something went.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哎,“海龟”小姐,今天的面试怎么样?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Āi, “hǎiguī” xiǎojiě, jīntiān de miànshì zěnmeyàng?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Hey, Ms. 'Sea Turtle,' how was your interview today?

highlight_words.explanation_en: An interjection used to get someone's attention, similar to 'Hey' or 'Oh'.
A question word used to ask about the condition or quality of something.

segments.visual_notes: Show a speech bubble from off-screen with the text inside. Highlight the question '怎么样?'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe feeling nervous and sweating.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker starts with 'Ha,' an exclamation of relief. She says [zh:开始有些紧张], meaning 'at the beginning, I was a bit nervous.' Then, [zh:出了好多汗], 'I sweated a lot.' The result? [zh:套装的上衣都快湿了] - 'The top of my suit was almost soaked.' Let's look at the key words: [zh:紧张] is 'nervous,' [zh:汗] is 'sweat,' and [zh:湿] means 'wet.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哈,开始有些紧张,出了好多汗,套装的上衣都快湿了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hā, kāishǐ yǒuxiē jǐnzhāng, chūle hǎoduō hàn, tàozhuāng de shàngyī dōu kuài shī le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Ha, I was a bit nervous at the beginning. I sweated so much that the top of my suit was almost soaked.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Describes a feeling of anxiety or tension.
The noun for perspiration. 'To sweat' is [zh:出汗].
A set of matching clothes, like a business suit.
The state of being soaked with liquid.

segments.visual_notes: Animate sweat drops appearing on a character's forehead.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask 'why' informally and reference past experiences.

narration.subtitle_en: Her friend asks a reasonable question. She starts with [zh:你已经不是第一次面试了], 'This isn't your first interview.' The phrase [zh:已经不是第一次] is very useful for talking about repeated experiences. Then she asks, [zh:干吗那么紧张], 'Why were you so nervous?' [zh:干吗] is an informal way to say 'why,' similar to 'what for?'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你已经不是第一次面试了,干吗那么紧张?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ yǐjīng búshì dì yī cì miànshì le, gànmá nàme jǐnzhāng?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: This isn't your first interview. Why were you so nervous?

highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates that an action or state has already occurred or been reached.
An informal interrogative used in spoken language to ask for the reason.

segments.visual_notes: Show a calendar with multiple 'interview' dates crossed off to illustrate 'not the first time'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe a person's role and demeanor.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's the reason for her nervousness. She explains, [zh:给我面试的是他们公司的总经理]. This means, 'The person who interviewed me was their company's general manager.' The key title here is [zh:总经理]. She adds that he was [zh:挺严肃的], which means 'very stern' or 'serious.' The word [zh:挺] is a common adverb for 'very'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 给我面试的是他们公司的总经理,挺严肃的,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Gěi wǒ miànshì de shì tāmen gōngsī de zǒngjīnglǐ, tǐng yánsù de,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The person who interviewed me was their general manager, he was very stern,

highlight_words.explanation_en: The head of a company. [zh:总] means 'general' or 'chief', and [zh:经理] is 'manager'.
Describes a person who is not smiling and has a formal, strict demeanor.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a manager with a very serious, unsmiling face.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a challenging interview question and its underlying assumption.

narration.subtitle_en: The manager's first sentence was very direct and challenging. [zh:第一句话就是] means 'His first sentence was...'. He asks, [zh:你是不是在美国找不到工作才回中国来的]. This question implies a skeptical assumption: 'Is it because you couldn't find a job in the US that you came back to China?' The structure [zh:是不是...才...] is often used to voice such a suspicion.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 第一句话就是:“你是不是在美国找不到工作才回中国来的?”
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: dì yī jù huà jiùshì: “Nǐ shìbushì zài Měiguó zhǎobudào gōngzuò cái huí Zhōngguó lái de?”
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: His first sentence was: 'Did you come back to China because you couldn't find a job in the United States?'

highlight_words.translation: is it or is it not
highlight_words.explanation_en: A common way to form a yes-no question, often implying an assumption.

highlight_words.translation: only then, then and only then
highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates that the second action happens only because the first condition is met.

segments.visual_notes: Show a map with an arrow moving from the US to China, with a question mark over it.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express empathy using 'no wonder'.

narration.subtitle_en: The friend completely understands the nervousness now. She exclaims, [zh:这个总经理可真的挺吓人的]! 'This general manager is really scary!' The word [zh:吓人] literally means 'to scare people.' Then she says, [zh:难怪你紧张], 'No wonder you were nervous.' [zh:难怪] is a great word to use when you understand the reason for something that was previously puzzling.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这个总经理可真的挺吓人的!难怪你紧张。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhège zǒngjīnglǐ kě zhēnde tǐng xiàrén de! Nánguài nǐ jǐnzhāng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: This general manager is really scary! No wonder you were nervous.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adjective used to describe something or someone that causes fear.
Used to express that something is not surprising after learning the reason.

segments.visual_notes: A lightbulb appears over the friend's head, showing she understands.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the '越...越...' structure for correlated changes.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker describes a downward spiral. She says, [zh:我越紧张就越解释不清楚]. This uses the important [zh:越...越...] structure, meaning 'the more... the more...'. So, 'The more nervous I got, the more unclear my explanation became.' The verb [zh:解释] means 'to explain,' and [zh:不清楚] means 'not clear' or 'incoherent.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我越紧张就越解释不清楚。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ yuè jǐnzhāng jiù yuè jiěshì bù qīngchu.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The more nervous I got, the less clearly I explained myself.
on_screen_text.notes: The structure 越 X 越 Y shows that Y changes along with X.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A pattern indicating a correlation where one quality or action increases or decreases in relation to another.
The act of making something clear or easy to understand.

segments.visual_notes: Show two arrows pointing up, one labeled 'Nervousness' and the other 'Unclear Explanation', to visualize the correlation.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe having a sudden idea and taking action.

narration.subtitle_en: Then, she had a breakthrough. [zh:后来我突然想出了一个主意]. 'Later, I suddenly had an idea.' [zh:突然] means 'suddenly,' and [zh:主意] is 'idea.' What was the idea? [zh:什么也不说了], 'I stopped talking,' and instead, she took action: [zh:从包里拿出两张纸,放到他前面], 'took out two pieces of paper from my bag, and placed them in front of him.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 后来我突然想出了一个主意,什么也不说了,从包里拿出两张纸,放到他前面。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hòulái wǒ tūrán xiǎngchūle yí ge zhǔyi, shénme yě bù shuō le, cóng bāo li náchū liǎng zhāng zhǐ, fàngdào tā qiánmiàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Later, I suddenly had an idea. I stopped talking, took out two pieces of paper from my bag, and placed them in front of him.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb describing an abrupt event.
A thought or suggestion for a course of action.
A resultative compound verb: [zh:拿] (to hold/take) + [zh:出] (out).

segments.visual_notes: Animate a lightbulb popping up, then a hand taking papers out of a bag.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn a metaphor for a slight improvement in someone's mood.

narration.subtitle_en: This is the manager's initial reaction. [zh:他一看], 'As soon as he saw them,' [zh:脸上马上阴转多云了], 'his expression immediately changed from overcast to partly cloudy.' This is a wonderful metaphor! [zh:阴转多云] is a phrase from weather forecasts. Here, it vividly describes his stern face softening slightly. His mood improved, but he wasn't fully convinced yet.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他一看,脸上马上阴转多云了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā yí kàn, liǎn shang mǎshàng yīn zhuǎn duō yún le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: As soon as he saw them, his expression immediately changed from overcast to partly cloudy.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to one's facial expression.
A metaphor for a person's mood or expression improving slightly.

segments.visual_notes: Show a face with a stern expression, which then morphs into a neutral one. An icon of an overcast sky changing to a partly cloudy sky appears next to it.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express wanting to prepare for something.

narration.subtitle_en: The friend is impressed and amused by this tactic. She asks, [zh:什么纸]? 'What kind of paper?' Then she jokes, [zh:我也去买两张准备着]. 'I'll go buy two sheets to have on hand, too.' The phrase [zh:准备着] means to prepare something and keep it ready for future use. The [zh:着] indicates the continuing state of being prepared.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 什么纸?我也去买两张准备着。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shénme zhǐ? Wǒ yě qù mǎi liǎng zhāng zhǔnbèizhe.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What kind of paper? I'll go buy two sheets to have on hand, too.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The general word for paper.
To get ready for something. The suffix [zh:着] implies a state of readiness.

segments.visual_notes: Show the friend winking and holding up two fingers.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the term for 'job offer letter'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's the reveal of her secret weapon. She explains they were [zh:录用通知] - 'job offer letters.' This is a crucial term for job hunting. [zh:录用] means 'to hire' or 'to accept for employment,' and [zh:通知] means 'notice.' So, a 'hiring notice.' These letters were from two companies in California, sent in May.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 是加州的两家公司五月寄给我的录用通知。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shì Jiāzhōu de liǎng jiā gōngsī wǔyuè jì gěi wǒ de lùyòng tōngzhī.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: They were job offer letters sent to me in May by two companies in California.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to sending something via post or courier.
A formal notification that a company has decided to hire you. [zh:录用] means 'to hire' and [zh:通知] is 'notice'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate two official-looking letters with 'Offer' stamped on them.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand questions starting with '既然' (since).

narration.subtitle_en: The manager sees the proof and his question changes. [zh:总经理看了接着问] - 'After the general manager looked at them, he then asked...'. His new question starts with [zh:既然]. This word means 'since' or 'given that,' and it introduces a premise that is now known to both parties. He asks, [zh:既然你...找到了工作, 为什么还要...], 'Since you've already found a job..., why do you still want to...?'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 总经理看了接着问:“既然你在美国公司找到了工作,为什么还要回中国工作呢?”
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zǒngjīnglǐ kàn le jiēzhe wèn: “Jìrán nǐ zài Měiguó gōngsī zhǎodào le gōngzuò, wèishéme hái yào huí Zhōngguó gōngzuò ne?”
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: After the general manager looked at them, he then asked, 'Since you've already found a job with an American company, why do you still want to come back to work in China?'

highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates that one action follows another in sequence.
A conjunction used to introduce a known fact or premise, from which a conclusion or question follows.

segments.visual_notes: Show the manager looking at the papers, and then a new, more thoughtful question bubble appearing.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the use of the final particle '嘛' to state the obvious.

narration.subtitle_en: The friend thinks the answer is simple. She says, [zh:这个问题不难回答], 'That question isn't hard to answer.' Her reason? [zh:你的男朋友在中国留学嘛]! 'Your boyfriend is studying in China!' The final particle [zh:嘛] is key here. It's used to state something the speaker considers to be obvious and self-evident. It adds a casual, 'of course!' tone to the sentence.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这个问题不难回答,你的男朋友在中国留学嘛!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhège wèntí bù nán huídá, nǐ de nánpéngyou zài Zhōngguó liúxué ma!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: That question isn't hard to answer. Your boyfriend is studying in China!

highlight_words.explanation_en: To respond to a question.
A sentence-final particle used to emphasize that the statement is obvious or self-evident.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a heart and a graduation cap in China, with the friend pointing to it as an 'obvious' answer.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to explain the potential negative consequence of an action.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker explains why her friend's 'obvious' answer isn't a good one for an interview. [zh:我要是这样说], 'If I had said that...'. She worries the manager [zh:很可能会以为], 'would most likely think...' that her job search in China was just a [zh:短期打算], a 'short-term plan.' The verb [zh:以为] often implies a mistaken assumption.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我要是这样说,他很可能会以为我在中国找工作,只是短期打算。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ yàoshi zhèyàng shuō, tā hěn kěnéng huì yǐwéi wǒ zài Zhōngguó zhǎo gōngzuò, zhǐshì duǎnqī dǎsuàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: If I had said that, he would most likely think I was only planning to work in China for a short term.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To think or believe something, which often turns out to be incorrect.
Literally 'short period'. The opposite is [zh:长期] (chángqī), 'long-term'.
Can be used as a noun for 'plan' or a verb for 'to plan'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a thought bubble from the manager with a calendar showing only a few months marked, indicating a 'short-term plan'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to use '好在' to mean 'fortunately'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here she notes a positive turn in her own state of mind. She says, [zh:好在这个时候我已经不紧张了]. The key phrase is [zh:好在], which means 'fortunately' or 'luckily.' It introduces a saving grace in a difficult situation. So, 'Fortunately, by this time I wasn't nervous anymore.' This confidence allowed her to give a much better answer.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 好在这个时候我已经不紧张了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hǎo zài zhège shíhou wǒ yǐjīng bù jǐnzhāng le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Fortunately, I wasn't nervous anymore by then.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to introduce a positive aspect or a fortunate turn of events in an otherwise negative or difficult situation.

segments.visual_notes: Show the character taking a deep breath and the 'nervous' squiggly lines around her disappearing.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe a structured explanation, moving from broad to specific.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, with her confidence back, she outlines her impressive answer. She uses a clear structure: [zh:我就从...谈到...再谈...]. This means 'I started by talking about..., then moved on to..., and then talked about...'. She begins with the big picture, [zh:中国经济发展] (China's economic development), then narrows down to their company's [zh:产品] (products), and finally to product [zh:销售] (sales).

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我就从中国经济发展,谈到他们公司的产品,再谈产品的销售,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: wǒ jiù cóng Zhōngguó jīngjì fāzhǎn, tándào tāmen gōngsī de chǎnpǐn, zài tán chǎnpǐn de xiāoshòu,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I started by talking about China's economic development, then about their company's products, and then about product sales,

highlight_words.explanation_en: [zh:经济] is 'economy' and [zh:发展] is 'development'.
Goods or services that are manufactured or sold.
The activity or business of selling products.

segments.visual_notes: Show a funnel diagram: 'China's Economy' at the top, 'Company Products' in the middle, and 'Sales' at the bottom.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to state the final point of an explanation.

narration.subtitle_en: This is the conclusion of her well-structured pitch. The word [zh:然后] means 'then' or 'after that,' signaling the final step in her explanation. She says she would [zh:解释为什么希望去他们公司工作], 'explain why I hoped to work for their company.' The word [zh:希望] means 'to hope' or 'to wish,' showing her genuine interest in the position.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 然后解释为什么希望去他们公司工作。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: ránhòu jiěshì wèishénme xīwàng qù tāmen gōngsī gōngzuò.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: and finally explained why I hoped to work for their company.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to indicate sequence, marking the next step in a process.
Expresses a desire for something to happen.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of the company logo with a heart over it to represent 'hope to work here'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn a metaphor for a significant improvement in mood and the 'Verb-ing, Verb-ing' pattern.

narration.subtitle_en: Here we see the final, successful outcome. The sentence uses the [zh:听着听着] pattern, which means 'as he listened on and on.' This 'Verb-ing, Verb-ing' structure suggests that while an action was in progress, something else happened. What happened? [zh:脸上多云转晴了]. His face changed 'from partly cloudy to sunny.' This weather metaphor, [zh:多云转晴], indicates his expression brightened up completely. He was won over.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 总经理听着听着,脸上多云转晴了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zǒngjīnglǐ tīngzhe tīngzhe, liǎn shang duō yún zhuǎn qíng le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: As the general manager listened, his face changed from partly cloudy to sunny.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A pattern (Verb + zhe + Verb + zhe) indicating an action is ongoing when another event occurs.
A metaphor for a person's mood or expression improving significantly, becoming bright and happy.

segments.visual_notes: Show the manager's face changing from neutral to a warm smile. A partly cloudy icon transitions to a bright sun icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand and differentiate two common weather metaphors used to describe changes in facial expressions.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's focus on the two creative phrases from this dialogue. Both are borrowed from weather forecasts. First, [zh:阴转多云], literally 'overcast turning to partly cloudy.' This describes a slight improvement in someone's mood, like when a stern face softens. Second, [zh:多云转晴], 'partly cloudy turning to sunny.' This indicates a much bigger, positive change, like when a neutral face breaks into a happy smile. They are a humorous and vivid way to talk about emotions.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 阴转多云 / 多云转晴
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yīn zhuǎn duō yún / duō yún zhuǎn qíng
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: from overcast to partly cloudy / from partly cloudy to sunny
on_screen_text.notes: Used metaphorically to describe changes in a person's facial expression.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Metaphor for a slight improvement in mood. [zh:阴] is overcast, [zh:多云] is partly cloudy.
Metaphor for a significant improvement in mood. [zh:晴] means sunny.

segments.visual_notes: Side-by-side comparison: On the left, an overcast icon changes to a partly cloudy one with a neutral face. On the right, a partly cloudy icon changes to a sunny one with a smiling face.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask if something is finished.

narration.subtitle_en: The friend asks a final question to wrap up the story: [zh:面试完了吧]? 'So the interview was over?' The verb [zh:完] means 'to finish.' Adding [zh:了] indicates the completion of the action. The final particle [zh:吧] softens the question and implies the speaker is making a guess or seeking confirmation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 面试完了吧?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Miànshì wán le ba?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: So the interview was over?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb or complement indicating the completion of an action.
A sentence-final particle that can indicate a suggestion, a softened command, or a supposition.

segments.visual_notes: Show a checkmark appearing next to the word 'Interview' on a to-do list.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a common interview question about weaknesses and the vocabulary used to ask it.

narration.subtitle_en: The interview isn't over. Xuemei says [zh:还没呢], meaning 'Not yet!'. Then, the general manager's action is described: [zh:这时候总经理突然笑着问]. This means 'Just then, the general manager suddenly smiled and asked'. The question he asks is a classic one in job interviews: [zh:你觉得你自己最大的缺点是什么], which translates to 'What do you think is your biggest weakness?'. The key word here is [zh:缺点], meaning 'weakness' or 'shortcoming'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 还没呢!这时候总经理突然笑着问:“你觉得你自己最大的缺点是什么?”
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hái méi ne! Zhè shíhou zǒngjīnglǐ tūrán xiàozhe wèn: “Nǐ juéde nǐ zìjǐ zuì dà de quēdiǎn shì shénme?”
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Not yet! Just then, the general manager suddenly smiled and asked, 'What do you think is your biggest weakness?'

highlight_words.explanation_en: The head of a company or a major division.
An adverb indicating an action happens unexpectedly or abruptly.
A fault or a point where something is lacking.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a question mark icon next to '缺点'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a friend's joking suggestion and learn the word for 'snacks'.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha responds with a joke. She starts with [zh:哈], which is the sound of a laugh. Then she says, [zh:我觉得], 'I think', followed by [zh:你最大的缺点], 'your biggest weakness', and her funny conclusion: [zh:就是爱吃零食], 'is that you love to eat snacks'. The word [zh:零食] refers to snacks like chips or candy. This is a lighthearted moment between friends.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哈,我觉得,你最大的缺点就是爱吃零食。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hā, wǒ juéde, nǐ zuì dà de quēdiǎn jiùshì ài chī língshí.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Ha, I think your biggest weakness is that you love to eat snacks.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to store-bought snacks like chips, candy, crackers, etc.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons of chips and candy next to the word '零食'.

segments.segment_title: Vocabulary: 零食 vs. 小吃
segments.teaching_goal: To differentiate between 'língshí' (packaged snacks) and 'xiǎochī' (light meals/street food).

narration.subtitle_en: Let's look closer at the word [zh:零食]. It's important to distinguish it from another word, [zh:小吃]. [zh:零食] refers to pre-packaged snacks like candies or chips that you'd buy in a store. On the other hand, [zh:小吃] refers to light, informal meals or street food, often made fresh and associated with a specific region. So, while both can be translated as 'snack', they are not interchangeable.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 零食 / 小吃
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: língshí / xiǎochī
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Snacks / Light Meals (Street Food)
on_screen_text.notes: 零食: Store-bought (chips, candy). 小吃: Freshly made (dumplings, noodles).

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to things like candies, crackers, melon seeds, and chips. Usually store-bought.
Informal, light meals, often made at home, in specialized restaurants, or at roadside food stands.

segments.visual_notes: Show a split screen: one side with bags of chips and candy for '零食', the other with a bowl of noodles or dumplings for '小吃'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a rhetorical question expressing concern about appearing unprofessional.

narration.subtitle_en: Xuemei expresses her concern about Lisha's joke. She asks a rhetorical question: [zh:那他不是要把我看成小女孩了?]. This means, 'Wouldn't he see me as a little girl then?'. The phrase [zh:看成] means 'to see as' or 'to regard as'. She's worried that such an answer would make her seem unprofessional or immature, like a [zh:小女孩], a 'little girl'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那他不是要把我看成小女孩了?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nà tā búshì yào bǎ wǒ kànchéng xiǎo nǚhái le?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Wouldn't he see me as a little girl then?

highlight_words.translation: to regard as, to see as
highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb indicating perception or consideration, treating someone or something as something else.
Used here to imply immaturity or a lack of seriousness.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a thought bubble above Xuemei's head showing a professional woman shaking her head at a childish drawing.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask 'how' something was done in the past.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha gets back to the main point and asks, [zh:那你是怎么回答的?]. This is a very common and useful phrase meaning, 'So how did you answer?'. The '是...的' structure is often used to emphasize the details of a past action, in this case, *how* she answered. The key verb is [zh:回答], 'to answer'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那你是怎么回答的?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nà nǐ shì zěnme huídá de?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: So how did you answer?

highlight_words.explanation_en: The verb for responding to a question or request.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the '是...的' structure in the sentence.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the first part of Xuemei's answer, where she defines an ideal professional.

narration.subtitle_en: Here begins Xuemei's actual, very clever answer. She starts by saying [zh:我说], 'I said', and then defines the ideal professional. She says a good [zh:销售管理人材], 'sales management professional', should be able to [zh:科学地安排自己的时间], 'scientifically arrange their own time'. This sets up a standard that she will then measure herself against.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我说,一个好的销售管理人材,应该能科学地安排自己的时间,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ shuō, yí ge hǎo de xiāoshòu guǎnlǐ réncái, yīnggāi néng kēxué de ānpái zìjǐ de shíjiān,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I said, a good sales management professional should be able to schedule their time scientifically.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The activity or business of selling products or services.
A talented person suitable for a specific role.
To organize or make plans for something.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a calendar or clock next to '安排自己的时间'.

segments.segment_title: Usage: 人材 vs. 人才
segments.teaching_goal: To learn that '人材' and '人才' are interchangeable words for 'talent'.

narration.subtitle_en: You might have noticed the word [zh:人材] in the last sentence. It can also be written as [zh:人才]. Both are pronounced the same and mean 'a person of ability' or 'talent'. The character [zh:材] means 'material', suggesting someone is the 'material' for a job. The character [zh:才] directly means 'talent'. For all practical purposes, these two words are interchangeable in modern Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 人材 vs. 人才
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: réncái vs. réncái
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: person of ability vs. person of ability

highlight_words.explanation_en: Lit. 'human material'. A person with the right qualities for a job.
Lit. 'human talent'. A talented individual.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how Xuemei contrasts herself with the ideal professional she described.

narration.subtitle_en: Xuemei continues her description. She says, 'People who are [zh:善于] (good at) working are [zh:往往] (often) also good at resting'. Then, she contrasts herself with this ideal by saying, [zh:我在这方面还很不够], 'I'm not quite good enough in this aspect'. This is a humble way to introduce her supposed 'weakness'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 善于工作的人往往也是善于休息的,我在这方面还很不够。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: shànyú gōngzuò de rén wǎngwǎng yěshì shànyú xiūxi de, wǒ zài zhè fāngmiàn hái hěn búgòu.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: People who are good at working are often also good at resting. I'm not quite good enough in this aspect.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates being skilled or adept at something.
Indicates something that usually happens under certain conditions.
To take a break or relax.

segments.visual_notes: Show a balance scale with '工作' on one side and '休息' on the other.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to work pressure and overtime.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, Xuemei provides a specific example. She says, 'During my [zh:实习] (internship), whenever I was under [zh:压力] (pressure) at work, I would [zh:熬夜加班] (stay up late and work overtime)'. The phrase [zh:熬夜] means to 'pull an all-nighter', and [zh:加班] means 'to work overtime'. This example cleverly showcases her dedication while framing it as a flaw.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 实习的时候,我工作一有压力就熬夜加班。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shíxí de shíhou, wǒ gōngzuò yì yǒu yālì jiù áoyè jiābān.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: During my internship, whenever I was under pressure, I would stay up late and work overtime.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A period of work experience offered by an organization for a limited period.
The feeling of being under mental or emotional strain.
To stay awake late into the night, especially to work or study.
To work additional hours beyond one's regular schedule.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a clock with hands spinning late into the night, next to a desk with a computer.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the conclusion of Xuemei's answer and the strategy behind it.

narration.subtitle_en: Finally, she concludes her answer. She starts with [zh:所以], meaning 'so'. Then she states her weakness: [zh:不太会休息], 'not being very good at resting', [zh:可能是我最大的缺点], 'is probably my biggest weakness'. By framing her strong work ethic as a weakness, she presents herself as a dedicated and self-aware candidate.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 所以,不太会休息可能是我最大的缺点。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Suǒyǐ, bú tài huì xiūxi kěnéng shì wǒ zuì dà de quēdiǎn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: So, not being good at taking breaks is probably my biggest weakness.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to introduce a result or conclusion.
An adverb or auxiliary verb expressing possibility.

segments.visual_notes: The word '缺点' (weakness) is shown, but then morphs into a thumbs-up icon or a muscle icon to show the hidden strength.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a question that points out the cleverness of an answer, and learn the word for 'strength'.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha's response shows she understands the strategy perfectly. She asks, 'Xuemei, are you actually talking about your weakness or your strength?'. The word [zh:到底] here means 'in the end' or 'after all', adding emphasis. The '是...还是...' structure presents a choice: is it a [zh:缺点] (weakness) or a [zh:优点] (strength)?

on_screen_text.focus_text: 雪梅,你到底是在说自己的缺点还是优点啊?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xuěméi, nǐ dàodǐ shì zài shuō zìjǐ de quēdiǎn háishì yōudiǎn a?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Xuemei, are you actually talking about your weakness or your strength?

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used in questions to press for a definitive answer.
The opposite of 缺点 (weakness); a positive quality or advantage.

segments.visual_notes: Show '缺点' and '优点' on opposite sides of the screen with a question mark in the middle.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a philosophical statement about personal growth and the '只要...就...' structure.

narration.subtitle_en: Xuemei reveals she said even more. She adds, [zh:我又说], 'I added'. She uses the [zh:只要...就...] structure, which means 'as long as... then...'. She says, 'As long as we can turn weaknesses into strengths, then weaknesses are not so [zh:可怕] (terrible/scary)'. The verb [zh:变成] means 'to turn into' or 'to become'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我又说,只要我们能把缺点变成优点,缺点就没那么可怕了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ yòu shuō, zhǐyào wǒmen néng bǎ quēdiǎn biànchéng yōudiǎn, quēdiǎn jiù méi nàme kěpà le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I added that as long as we can turn our weaknesses into strengths, weaknesses are not so terrible.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction that introduces a condition, used with 就.
Indicates a change or transformation.
Causing fear or dread.

segments.visual_notes: Animate an arrow looping from '缺点' to '优点'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the description of the manager's positive actions and the '一边...一边...' pattern.

narration.subtitle_en: This part describes the manager's very positive reaction. [zh:说到这儿] means 'at this point'. The manager [zh:站起来] (stood up). The sentence then uses the '一边...一边...' pattern to describe two actions happening at the same time: he was shaking her hand, [zh:握手], while smiling and speaking. This is a strong sign of a successful interview.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 说到这儿,总经理站起来,一边跟我握手,一边笑着说,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shuō dào zhèr, zǒngjīnglǐ zhàn qǐlái, yìbiān gēn wǒ wòshǒu, yìbiān xiàozhe shuō,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: At this point, the general manager stood up, shaking my hand while smiling and saying,

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common professional and social gesture. Lit. 'hold hand'.
A pattern used to indicate two actions are happening simultaneously.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight '一边' and '一边' and show two simultaneous actions (shaking hands, smiling).

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the positive outcome of the interview and related vocabulary.

narration.subtitle_en: Here is what the manager said. [zh:他相信我的能力] means 'he believed in my ability'. The verb [zh:相信] is 'to believe' or 'to trust', and [zh:能力] is 'ability'. Then, he said [zh:让我等他们的好消息], which means 'he told me to wait for their good news'. Being told to wait for [zh:好消息] (good news) is a very promising outcome.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他相信我的能力,让我等他们的好消息。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: tā xiāngxìn wǒ de nénglì, ràng wǒ děng tāmen de hǎo xiāoxi.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: he believed in my abilities and that I should wait for their good news.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To have faith or confidence in someone or something.
The power or skill to do something.
Positive or favorable information.

segments.visual_notes: Animate an envelope icon with a checkmark on it for '好消息'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to congratulate someone and understand a playful joke.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha is thrilled for her friend. She exclaims, [zh:太棒了], which means 'That's great!' or 'Awesome!'. Then she says [zh:恭喜你], 'Congratulations to you'. She finishes with a playful title, [zh:林经理] or 'Manager Lin', jokingly promoting Xuemei to the position already. [zh:经理] is the word for 'manager'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 太棒了!恭喜你,林经理!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tài bàng le! Gōngxǐ nǐ, Lín jīnglǐ!

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the '越...越...' (yuè...yuè...) structure to express that one thing changes in accordance with another.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, let's dive into our first grammar point: the structure [zh:越] X [zh:越] Y. This pattern means 'the more X, the more Y.' It shows that the degree of Y changes along with the degree of X. In the dialogue, Xuemei said, [zh:我越紧张就越解释不清楚]. This means 'The more nervous I got, the less clearly I could explain.' Notice how her nervousness directly impacted her ability to speak clearly. Here's another example: [zh:你越说我越不懂], which means 'The more you explain, the less I understand.' This is a very useful pattern to describe escalating situations or feelings.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 越 + Adj./Verb, 越 + Adj./Verb
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yuè + Adj./Verb, yuè + Adj./Verb
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The more..., the more...

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure used to indicate that the second quality or action changes in degree along with the first.

grammar_points.pattern: 我越紧张就越解释不清楚。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒ yuè jǐnzhāng jiù yuè jiěshì bù qīngchu.
grammar_points.explanation_en: The more nervous I got, the more incoherent my explanation became.

grammar_points.pattern: 车越开越快。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Chē yuè kāi yuè kuài.
grammar_points.explanation_en: The car is driven faster and faster.

grammar_points.pattern: 越早越好。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Yuè zǎo yuè hǎo.
grammar_points.explanation_en: The earlier, the better.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the use of '既然' (jìrán) to introduce a known fact or premise, followed by a logical conclusion or question.

narration.subtitle_en: Next up is the conjunction [zh:既然]. We use [zh:既然] to start a sentence when we want to state a reason or premise that is already known to both the speaker and the listener. The second part of the sentence then presents a logical conclusion or question based on that premise. For instance, the manager asked Xuemei, [zh:既然你在美国公司找到了工作,为什么还要回中国工作呢]. This translates to 'Since you've already found a job with an American company, why do you still want to come back to work in China?' The manager is using the known fact of her US job offer to ask a logical follow-up question. Remember, while it indicates a reason, it's different from [zh:因为] because [zh:既然] emphasizes a shared understanding of the premise.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 既然 + Premise, + Conclusion
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: jìrán + Premise, + Conclusion
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Since..., ...
on_screen_text.notes: The premise is a fact already known to the listener.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to introduce a premise that is already known, leading to a logical conclusion or inference.

grammar_points.pattern: 既然你在美国公司找到了工作,为什么还要回中国工作呢?
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Jìrán nǐ zài Měiguó gōngsī zhǎodào le gōngzuò, wèishéme hái yào huí Zhōngguó gōngzuò ne?
grammar_points.explanation_en: Since you've already found a job with an American company, why do you still want to come back to work in China?

grammar_points.pattern: 既然你病了,就换个时间吧。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Jìrán nǐ bìng le, jiù huàn ge shíjiān ba.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Since you're sick, let's find another time.

segments.visual_notes: Use a 'lightbulb' icon appearing over the speaker's head to signify the known premise, followed by a question mark or an arrow pointing to the conclusion.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the 'Verb + 着 + Verb + 着' pattern, which indicates an unexpected event occurring during an ongoing action.

narration.subtitle_en: Our final grammar point is a dynamic one: 'Verb [zh:着] Verb [zh:着]'. This structure describes an action that is ongoing, and then, surprisingly or unintentionally, a second action happens. In our story, Xuemei says the manager [zh:听着听着,脸上多云转晴了]. This means 'As the general manager listened and listened, his gloomy face gradually brightened up.' The listening was continuous, and the change in his expression was the surprising result. The adverb [zh:就] is often used before the second verb. For example, [zh:那个孩子哭着哭着就睡着了] means 'The child cried and cried and then fell asleep.' The sleeping was an unintended consequence of the crying.

on_screen_text.focus_text: Verb + 着 + Verb + 着, (就) + Unintended Result
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Verb + zhe + Verb + zhe, (jiù) + Unintended Result
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: While doing something, something else happens unexpectedly.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A pattern indicating that while an action is ongoing, a second, often surprising, action occurs.

grammar_points.pattern: 总经理听着听着,脸上多云转晴了。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Zǒngjīnglǐ tīngzhe tīngzhe, liǎnshang duōyún zhuǎn qíng le.
grammar_points.explanation_en: As the general manager listened, his gloomy face gradually brightened up.

grammar_points.pattern: 那个孩子哭着哭着就睡着了。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Nàge háizi kūzhe kūzhe jiù shuì zháo le.
grammar_points.explanation_en: The child cried and cried and (then) fell asleep.

segments.visual_notes: Show a continuous action (like listening) with a looping animation, then have a surprise element (like a smiley face appearing) pop up.

segments.teaching_goal: To review the key grammar patterns and vocabulary from the lesson.

narration.subtitle_en: Great job! We've covered some very useful grammar today. We learned how to say 'the more, the more' with [zh:越...越...], how to form conclusions based on known facts with [zh:既然], and how to describe surprising events with 'Verb [zh:着] Verb [zh:着]'. Keep practicing these patterns, and you'll sound more and more fluent. See you next time!

segments.teaching_goal: Comprendre le contexte de la croissance économique de la Chine et l'afflux d'entreprises multinationales, et apprendre le vocabulaire associé.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette première phrase plante le décor de notre histoire. Elle indique que depuis le début du 21e siècle, l'économie chinoise s'est considérablement développée. Décortiquons-la. [zh:21世纪初以来] signifie « depuis le début du 21e siècle ». [zh:经济] veut dire « économie », et [zh:发展] signifie « développement ». En conséquence, de nombreuses [zh:跨国公司], c'est-à-dire des « entreprises multinationales », sont [zh:进入] « entrées » en Chine. Ce boom économique a créé de nombreuses nouvelles opportunités d'emploi.

content.focus_text: 21世纪初以来,中国的经济有了很大的发展,不少跨国公司进入中国。
content.focus_pinyin: 21 shìjì chū yǐlái, Zhōngguó de jīngjì yǒule hěn dà de fāzhǎn, bù shǎo kuàguó gōngsī jìnrù Zhōngguó.
content.focus_gloss_en: Depuis le début du 21e siècle, l'économie chinoise s'est considérablement développée, et de nombreuses entreprises multinationales sont entrées en Chine.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Désigne une période de 100 ans.
Un adjectif qui décrit quelque chose qui opère dans différents pays. Il est formé de [zh:跨] (traverser) et [zh:国] (pays).
Un verbe qui signifie entrer dans un lieu ou un état.

segments.teaching_goal: Pour expliquer qui a été attiré par ces entreprises et introduire le vocabulaire lié aux talents et aux étudiants étrangers.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette phrase explique qui a été attiré par ces nouveaux emplois. Elle dit que ces grandes entreprises ont [zh:吸引] « attiré » beaucoup de [zh:人材], c'est-à-dire des « talents », venus de [zh:世界各地], soit « du monde entier ». Ce groupe comprend des étudiants chinois qui ont étudié [zh:在国外], donc « à l'étranger », et qui ont obtenu leur diplôme. Le mot [zh:留学生] désigne spécifiquement les « étudiants qui étudient à l'étranger » ou les « étudiants étrangers ».

content.focus_text: 这些大公司吸引了世界各地不少的人材，包括一些在国外学习毕业的中国留学生。
content.focus_pinyin: Zhèxiē dà gōngsī xīyǐnle shìjiè gèdì bùshǎo de réncái, bāokuò yīxiē zài guówài xuéxí bìyè de Zhōngguó liúxuéshēng.
content.focus_gloss_en: Ces grandes entreprises ont attiré de nombreux talents du monde entier, y compris des étudiants chinois diplômés de leurs études à l'étranger.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Un verbe qui signifie attirer ou tirer vers soi.
Une personne douée ou talentueuse. Nous allons examiner ce mot plus en détail ensuite.
Littéralement 'hors du pays', cela signifie les pays étrangers ou l'outre-mer.

segments.segment_title: Vocabulaire : 人材 vs. 人才
segments.teaching_goal: Pour comprendre l'interchangeabilité et la subtile différence entre 人材 et 人才.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Regardons de plus près le mot pour « talent ». Dans le texte, vous voyez [zh:人材]. Vous pourriez aussi le voir écrit [zh:人才]. En pratique, ils sont interchangeables. Le caractère [zh:材] signifie littéralement « bois d'œuvre » ou « matériau ». Il suggère qu'une personne est une ressource précieuse pour une entreprise ou un pays. Le caractère [zh:才] signifie directement « talent » ou « capacité ». Les deux se réfèrent à une personne talentueuse.

content.focus_text: 人材 / 人才
content.focus_gloss_en: personne de talent

highlight_words.explanation_en: Une personne de talent. Peut aussi s'écrire 人才.

content.notes: 材 [zh:cái] signifie « matériau », tandis que 才 [zh:cái] signifie « talent ». Les deux sont utilisés.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Une personne douée. Peut aussi s'écrire 人才.

segments.teaching_goal: Présenter et expliquer le terme chinois moderne « 海归 » [zh:hǎiguī] désignant les personnes de retour de l'étranger.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette phrase présente un terme chinois moderne très important. Elle explique que lorsque ces étudiants [zh:归来] « reviennent » de [zh:海外] « l'étranger ». On les [zh:被叫做] « appelle » des [zh:海归]. Ce mot est une combinaison astucieuse de [zh:海] qui vient de « l'étranger » et de [zh:归] qui signifie « revenir ». Il désigne spécifiquement les Chinois qui sont rentrés en Chine après avoir étudié ou travaillé à l'étranger.

content.focus_text: 他们从海外归来，被叫做“海归”。
content.focus_pinyin: Tāmen cóng hǎiwài guīlái, bèi jiàozuò “hǎiguī”.
content.focus_gloss_en: Quand ils reviennent de l'étranger, on les appelle des « hǎiguī » (rapatriés).

highlight_words.explanation_en: Littéralement « en dehors des mers », cela signifie à l'étranger ou outre-mer.
Une manière formelle de dire « revenir » ou « retourner ».
Utilisé pour introduire un nom ou un terme pour quelque chose.
Littéralement « en dehors des mers », cela signifie à l'étranger ou outre-mer.

segments.segment_title: Le jeu de mots avec « 海龟 »
segments.teaching_goal: Présenter le jeu de mots « 海龟 » [zh:hǎiguī] (tortue de mer) et comprendre son lien avec « 海归 » [zh:hǎiguī].

narration_track.subtitle_en: Passons maintenant à un peu d'humour chinois. Le texte dit que certaines personnes les appellent en plaisantant [zh:海龟]. Pourquoi ? Le mot pour « tortue de mer », [zh:海龟], se prononce exactement comme le mot pour « rapatrié », [zh:海归]. C'est un jeu de mots, un aspect courant et amusant de la langue chinoise. L'expression [zh:开玩笑] signifie « plaisanter » ou « blaguer ».

content.focus_text: 有的人还开玩笑地把他们叫做“海龟”。
content.focus_pinyin: Yǒu de rén hái kāi wánxiào de bǎ tāmen jiàozuò “hǎiguī”.
content.focus_gloss_en: Certaines personnes les appellent aussi en plaisantant des « hǎiguī » [zh:hǎiguī] (tortues de mer).

highlight_words.explanation_en: Un jeu de mots avec « 海归 » [zh:hǎiguī] (rapatrié) car ils ont la même prononciation.
Une expression courante qui signifie faire une blague ou plaisanter.

segments.segment_title: Utilisation : 海归 vs. 海龟
segments.teaching_goal: Comprendre le contexte culturel du jeu de mots sur '海龟' et son opposé, '土鳖'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Approfondissons ce jeu de mots. [zh:海归] désigne une personne très instruite qui revient de l'étranger. Le jeu de mots [zh:海龟], qui signifie « tortue de mer », est un surnom amusant et très utilisé. Il existe même un terme opposé : [zh:土鳖], qui signifie littéralement « tortue molle rustique ». Les personnes qui n'ont jamais étudié à l'étranger peuvent utiliser ce terme pour se décrire de manière auto-dérisoire. C'est un excellent exemple d'argot chinois moderne.

content.focus_text: 海归 (hǎiguī) vs. 海龟 (hǎiguī)
content.focus_gloss_en: Rapatrié vs. Tortue de mer (Jeu de mots)
content.notes: Opposé : 土鳖 (tǔbiē) – 'tortue rustique', quelqu'un qui n'est pas allé à l'étranger.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Une personne qui est revenue de l'étranger, généralement après avoir étudié.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Voici notre personnage principal. La phrase dit : « Lin Xuemei est maintenant l'une de ces nouvelles tortues de mer. » Remarquez le classificateur utilisé ici : [zh:一只]. Ce classificateur est généralement employé pour les animaux. L'utiliser ici avec [zh:海龟] renforce le jeu de mots avec « tortue de mer ». Cela ajoute une touche ludique à la description.

content.focus_text: 林雪梅现在就是其中的一只新“海龟”。

segments.teaching_goal: Décrire les actions de Lin Xuemei après son retour en Chine et apprendre du vocabulaire lié à la recherche d'emploi.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette phrase décrit ce que Xuemei a fait après son retour en Chine. [zh:以后不久] signifie « peu de temps après ». Donc, peu après son retour, elle a immédiatement commencé sa recherche d'emploi. La phrase énumère trois actions clés. D'abord, [zh:上网] pour « aller en ligne ». Ensuite, [zh:查资料] pour « chercher des informations ». Et enfin, [zh:找工作] pour « chercher un emploi ».

content.focus_text: 雪梅回到中国以后不久,就开始上网查资料、找工作。

content.focus_gloss_en: Peu de temps après son retour en Chine, Xuemei a commencé à chercher des informations et à chercher un emploi en ligne.

La particule [zh:过] après le verbe indique une expérience passée. Cependant, la phrase dit qu'elle [zh:都不太满意]. Cela signifie qu'elle n'était « pas très satisfaite d'aucune d'entre elles ». Le mot clé ici est [zh:满意], qui signifie « satisfait ».

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette phrase est un peu longue, mais elle contient l'événement clé. Décomposons-la. « Hier, une grande entreprise qu'elle aime beaucoup... » [zh:昨天有一家她非常喜欢的大公司]. « ...l'a invitée à venir ce matin pour un entretien... » [zh:叫她今天上午去面试]. Le poste est pour un [zh:销售经理], un « directeur des ventes ». Le mot [zh:面试] signifie « entretien d'embauche », un mot très important pour cette leçon.

segments.teaching_goal: Pour situer le dialogue et comprendre la structure « 刚...就... ».

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette phrase situe le moment de l'appel téléphonique. [zh:面试以后] signifie « après l'entretien ». La structure [zh:刚...就...] est très utile. Elle signifie « dès que... alors... ». Donc, « dès qu'elle est rentrée chez elle, elle a reçu un appel de Lisa ». L'expression verbale [zh:接到电话] signifie « recevoir un appel téléphonique ».

content.focus_text: 面试以后,刚回到家里就接到丽莎的电话。
